site.name: Govardhan Reddy Narala
site.headline: Software Engineer with 3 years of experience building event-driven backend systems and distributed platforms processing 50M+ monthly transactions at 99.9% SLA.
site.heroIntro: Software Engineer with 3 years of experience building event-driven backend systems and distributed platforms processing 50M+ monthly transactions at 99.9% SLA.
heroSummary: Specialize in APIs, data pipelines, and scalable systems with measurable performance impact.

This portfolio is organized around proof: 50M+ monthly records, 99.9% SLA systems, 400% traffic spikes, sub-45-minute incident response, and project work spanning backend services, full-stack platforms, and automation.
heroProofPoints: Built systems processing 50M+ monthly records and 10M+ monthly transactions at 99.9% SLA.
Reduced SQL query latency by 45% across data-intensive services and lowered MTTR to under 45 minutes.
Shipped full-stack and distributed systems using .NET, Node.js, Next.js, Kafka-style event flows, GraphQL, Redis, and Docker.
Combined backend engineering depth with analytics, ML research, observability, and automation.

homeValueCards.title: Product To Platform
homeValueCards.copy: I can work from frontend and user-facing flows down to service boundaries, data contracts, infrastructure, and operational behavior.

homeValueCards.title: Reliability Under Load
homeValueCards.copy: My background is grounded in production systems, so I design for uptime, observability, throughput, operational clarity, and failure handling from the start.

homeValueCards.title: Engineering Plus ML
homeValueCards.copy: I do not treat ML as a disconnected research artifact. I connect modeling, data pipelines, backend systems, and automation into end-to-end delivery.

aboutSections.title: How I Position Myself
aboutSections.copy: I position myself as a software engineer with strong backend, data, and ML-systems depth. That means I can build APIs, data pipelines, distributed workflows, and the supporting reliability layer around them.

aboutSections.title: Why This Portfolio Is Different
aboutSections.copy: The work here is not limited to toy apps or isolated notebooks. It shows enterprise backend systems, full-stack platform work, event-driven integrations, observability, and research-backed ML projects presented with real engineering context.

aboutSections.title: Best Role Fit
aboutSections.copy: I am strongest in software engineer, full-stack, backend, data engineering, and ML-systems roles where system quality, measurable outcomes, and end-to-end ownership matter.

currentStatus.title: Open To Work
currentStatus.copy: Actively pursuing Software Engineer, Full Stack, Backend, Data Engineering, and ML-driven systems opportunities.

currentStatus.title: What I Want To Build
currentStatus.copy: Production-grade platforms, backend services, and data-ML systems that combine reliability, scalability, and practical user value.

currentStatus.title: Working Style
currentStatus.copy: I am most effective in environments where I can bridge engineering and analytics, work through ambiguity, and turn complex data problems into clear delivery.

projectOverrides.title: Realtime Commerce Platform
projectOverrides.summary: Real-time event-driven commerce platform built as an 11-service ecosystem with GraphQL BFF, Redis, recommendations, and full cart-to-fulfillment lifecycle orchestration.
highlights: Architected an 11-service microservices ecosystem using Node.js, Next.js, .NET, FastAPI, GraphQL, and Redis around a real-time commerce domain.
Managed the full cart-to-fulfillment lifecycle with event-driven order processing, real-time inventory flows, customer storefront experience, and admin-side operational views.
Combined gateway, recommendations, search, caching, and asynchronous messaging patterns into a more production-like marketplace platform.

projectOverrides.title: LinkedIn Outreach Agent
projectOverrides.summary: LLM-powered Chrome extension that reads live LinkedIn context and drafts profile-grounded outreach with browser automation and human review before send.
highlights: Built a Chrome extension that reads visible LinkedIn context and drafts recruiter replies, follow-ups, and connection outreach grounded in profile context.
Supported OpenAI, Ollama, and OpenAI-compatible local LLM servers so the tool can run in either cloud or privacy-first local mode.
Added browser automation for repetitive flows while keeping a review-first design so every message remains human-approved.

projectOverrides.title: CivicQuest
projectOverrides.summary: AI-supported community engagement platform for volunteering discovery, sustainability learning, and guided interaction.
highlights: AI-powered community engagement platform
Volunteer discovery and registration journeys
Gamified sustainability learning plus Azure bot integration

projectOverrides.title: Disease Networks for Disease Clustering
projectOverrides.summary: End-to-end graph learning pipeline that combines disease-gene networks with Gene Ontology features to improve unsupervised biomedical disease clustering.
highlights: Built disease x gene and disease x GO feature matrices to combine graph topology with biological process semantics.
Trained a 3-layer GCN encoder and clustered learned latent embeddings with KMeans for stronger disease-group separation.
Improved inter-vs-intra cluster distance separation from 0.90 to 1.45, 0.85 to 1.25, and 1.10 to 1.85 across 40, 50, and 60-disease subsets.

projectOverrides.title: Zero Hunger Big Data Analytics Pipeline
projectOverrides.summary: Big-data pipeline aligned to UN SDG 2 that processes 86,657 records across hunger, agriculture, and socio-economic indicators into scalable analytics and forecasting workflows.
highlights: Designed ingestion, transformation, and querying workflows across HDFS, Hadoop Streaming MapReduce, Spark, and Hive.
Normalized public data sources including FAO, World Bank, and UNICEF-style datasets into analysis-ready tables for hunger hotspot detection.
Built forecasting and visualization workflows, with project materials reporting about 95% prediction accuracy for hunger-trend evaluation.

projectOverrides.title: Houston Crime Type Classification (2010-2024)
projectOverrides.summary: Large-scale crime analytics pipeline on roughly 3.6M Houston crime records to predict NIBRS offense categories under a realistic temporal holdout design.
highlights: Standardized and merged three differently formatted source datasets into one modeling-ready schema spanning 2010 to 2024.
Engineered Beat, Premise, Month, Weekday, and Hour Group features and cleaned multi-label crime records into separate observations.
Selected MLPClassifier as the best model, reaching 29.37% accuracy and 0.5895 macro AUC on the 2024 holdout set.

projectOverrides.title: Multimodal Book Genre Classification and Clustering
projectOverrides.summary: Multimodal content-intelligence pipeline that combines book titles and cover images to classify and segment books across large catalog datasets.
highlights: Built a dual-modality workflow using Word2Vec and LSTM for titles plus CNN-based visual features for covers across Book32 and BookCover30.
Performed large-scale preprocessing and missing-data analysis before classification and clustering.
Validated genre separation with KMeans and PCA, surfacing interpretable clusters such as Self-Help, Religion and Spirituality, and Education.

projectOverrides.title: Adversarial Attacks on Deep Neural Networks
projectOverrides.summary: Medical imaging robustness benchmark evaluating adversarial attacks and defenses across four CNN architectures on the MURA X-ray dataset.
highlights: Benchmarked four CNN architectures on 40,561 MURA X-ray images across multiple perturbation levels using FGSM, PGD, and Carlini-Wagner attacks.
Built a preprocessing workflow with resizing, normalization, CLAHE enhancement, bone windowing, and smart cropping for clinically consistent inputs.
Implemented FGSM adversarial training with 50% clean and 50% adversarial batches over 50 epochs, improving FGSM robustness and yielding moderate PGD gains.

projectOverrides.title: Boomi CI/CD Experiments
projectOverrides.summary: Enterprise integration and reliability work around Boomi-based distributed workflows, runtime observability, and support automation.
highlights: Supported 10M+ daily transactions across distributed runtimes while sustaining 99.9% SLA uptime for business-critical workflows.
Implemented validation and control checks across 6 ingestion pipelines to reduce downstream failures and improve data consistency.
Automated health checks, restart workflows, and runbooks to eliminate recurring failures and reduce onboarding time for new engineers.

projectOverrides.title: Node E-Commerce Use Case
projectOverrides.summary: API-oriented e-commerce use case covering shopping flows, backend logic, and service design basics.
highlights: Implemented core e-commerce backend flows
Used API-first design for shopping interactions
Built a foundation for larger full-stack demos

projectOverrides.title: Microservices Using .NET Core
projectOverrides.summary: Clean-architecture microservices system using messaging, RPC, caching, and multiple persistence layers.
highlights: Designed loosely coupled services with clean architecture
Used RabbitMQ and gRPC for inter-service communication
Applied cache and polyglot persistence patterns

projectOverrides.title: ASP.NET Core Docs (Fork)
projectOverrides.summary: Reference fork of ASP.NET Core documentation used for study and internal notes.
highlights: Maintained as a working reference repo
Useful for .NET architecture and API patterns
Supports learning and implementation review

projectOverrides.title: deploy.github.io
projectOverrides.summary: Minimal static deployment experiment for lightweight hosting and GitHub Pages-style publication.
highlights: Lightweight static deployment setup
Useful baseline for simple hosting workflows
Small experiment in publishing and delivery
